title: Sinter Plant
point: Sinter plant agglomerates iron ore fines into porous sinter

title: Sintering
point: Sintering is agglomeration by incipient fusion using heat

title: Purpose of Sintering
point: Converts waste fines into blast furnace burden

title: Technology Origin
point: Sintering technology developed in early 20th century

title: Preferred Burden
point: Sinter is preferred blast furnace burden material

title: Charge Preparation
point: Raw materials are proportioned by charge calculation

title: Balling Drum Function
point: Mixes base mix with water and forms balls

title: Green Mix
point: Nodulized base mix ready for sintering

title: Hearth Layer
point: Hearth layer uses 10–20 mm finished sinter

title: Charging
point: Green mix is loaded above hearth layer

title: Ignition
point: Top layer ignited in ignition furnace

title: Air Flow
point: Air is drawn downward by exhausters

title: Sinter
point: Strong porous aggregate formed after sintering

title: End of Sintering
point: Sintering ends when bottom coke burning is completed

title: Post Treatment
point: Sinter cake is crushed cooled and screened

title: Ideal Sinter Size
point: Blast furnace sinter size is 5–40 mm

title: Return Fines
point: -5 mm sinter is recycled to sinter bins

title: Under Grate Suction
point: Fines are sintered using suction through grates

title: Fuel Type
point: Finely crushed coke acts as fuel

title: Ignition Furnace
point: Gaseous fuel burner used for ignition

title: COG
point: Coke oven gas is used in ignition furnace

title: Mixed Gas
point: Mixed gas contains COG and BF gas

title: Reaction Type
point: Combustion zone has thermal and chemical reactions

title: Reaction Type
point: Other zones have mainly thermal reactions

title: Zone Movement
point: Zone speed depends on sintering speed

title: Heat Transfer
point: Ready sinter transfers heat to sucked air

title: High Temperature Formation
point: Hot air and preheated charge create peak temperature

title: Sinter Formation
point: Physical and chemical changes form sinter

title: Moisture Zone
point: Exhaust gases transfer moisture to charge

title: Moisture Effect
point: Temperature stays low until moisture removal

title: Ore Requirement
point: 750–825 kg ore fines per ton sinter

title: +10 mm Ore
point: +10 mm iron ore should be nil

title: Flux Crushing Index
point: -3 mm flux fraction should be at least 90%

title: Coke Crushing Index
point: -3 mm coke fraction should be at least 85%

title: Moisture Role
point: Water acts as binder in base mix

title: Cooling
point: Cooled by forced draft air blowers

title: Cooling Practice
point: Larger sinter should remain at bottom

title: Cooling Practice
point: Smaller sinter should remain at top

title: Advantage
point: Utilizes iron ore fines from mines

title: Advantage
point: Flux calcination saves blast furnace coke

title: Advantage
point: Reduces sulfur phosphorus zinc and alkali effects

title: Advantage
point: Higher softening temperature than burden materials

title: RDI Use
point: Predicts degradation in blast furnace stack

title: BTP Location Detail
point: Found near discharge end wind box

title: Specific Productivity
point: Sinter produced per square meter per hour

title: Gas Safety
point: Mixed gas and COG require strict protocols

title: Gas Safety PPE
point: Use gas mask during gas line work

title: ISO 45001:2018
point: Occupational Health and Safety Management System

title: Sinter Formula
point: Fines plus fuel plus heat plus suction equals sinter

title: Hearth Layer Purpose
point: Protects pallets and improves bed permeability
